back_translation: For the choirmaster. For Jeduthun. A psalm of David.
I said, 'I will watch my ways, so as not to sin with my tongue; I will guard my mouth with a muzzle as long as the wicked are before me.'
I was mute and still; I kept silent even from speaking good, and my pain was stirred up.
My heart grew hot within me; as I mused, the fire burned. Then I spoke with my tongue:
O LORD, show me my end and the measure of my days; let me know how fleeting my life is.
Behold, You have made my days a few handbreadths, and my lifetime is as nothing before You. Surely everyone, even at his most secure, is but a breath. Selah
Surely man goes about like a shadow; surely he bustles about in vain; he heaps up wealth and does not know who will gather it.
And now, O Lord, for what do I wait? My hope is in You.
Deliver me from all my transgressions; do not make me the reproach of fools.
I am mute; I do not open my mouth, for it is You who have done it.
Remove Your scourge from me; I am perishing by the blows of Your hand.
You discipline and correct man for his iniquity; You make what he treasures dissolve like a moth. Surely every man is but a vapor. Selah
Hear my prayer, O LORD, and listen to my cry for help; do not be silent at my tears. For I am a foreigner dwelling with You, a stranger like all my fathers.
Turn Your gaze away from me, that I may regain joy before I depart and am no more.
